message:
perf(core): cache autocommit publication binding across consecutive file-backed begins

Add a per-Connection cache for the BoundPagerPublication used by the
file-backed autocommit fast path, keyed on the pager's finalized commit
epoch plus the schema cookie.  Previously every `autocommit_begin`
re-bound the pager publication end-to-end (page-cache scan + snapshot
refresh) even when no writer had produced a new commit since the last
autocommit, which shows up as a fat bar in the FrankenSQLite hot-path
profile on read-heavy workloads with many statement cycles.

Mechanism:
- New Cell<Option<BoundPagerPublication>> fields on Connection alongside
  their matching epoch/cookie guards:
    cached_autocommit_publication
    cached_autocommit_publication_epoch
    cached_autocommit_publication_cookie
  All initialised to `None` / `0` in both Connection::open and
  Connection::open_with_pager. Also add
  `cached_read_snapshot_commit_epoch` so the parked-reader invalidation
  logic can compare against the finalized commit epoch in addition to
  the schema cookie.
- `cached_autocommit_publication(scenario_id)` returns `Some(publication)`
  iff (a) scenario is `autocommit_begin`, (b) backing is file-backed
  (not :memory:), (c) the cached epoch matches `current_global_commit_seq`,
  (d) the cached cookie matches `schema_cookie`, and (e) the cached
  publication's snapshot is not ahead of either the global commit seq
  or the per-memdb visible-commit pointer.  Any mismatch falls through
  to the full bind path, guaranteeing we never serve stale snapshots.
- `remember_autocommit_publication(scenario_id, publication)` installs
  the cache after a successful full bind; before storing it re-checks
  the snapshot-vs-epoch guard and invalidates if the new publication
  is already ahead of the commit seq (can only happen across concurrent
  writers), keeping us safe under races.
- `autocommit_begin` short-circuits to the cached publication when
  `cached_autocommit_publication` returns `Some`, otherwise it runs the
  usual bind path and then calls `remember_autocommit_publication`.

Guarded constraints (cache is skipped entirely in these cases):
- :memory: databases — the in-memory autocommit fast path already has
  its own `cached_write_txn` reuse path and different invariants.
- Non-autocommit-begin scenarios — long-running explicit transactions
  need their own binding lifecycle.
- Any snapshot/epoch/cookie mismatch — cache is invalidated and fully
  refreshed, so correctness cannot degrade even if cache bookkeeping
  has a bug.

Test: adds an inline unit test that drives two consecutive file-backed
autocommit begins against a freshly opened connection and asserts
`hot_path_profile.pager_publication_refreshes == 1` after both begins
(instead of 2), proving the second begin hits the cache.
